name: neurokit2
tagline: Reference/how-to skill for the neurokit2 Python library covering ECG/EEG/EDA/RSP/EMG/EOG biosignal processing workflows.
verdict: Clean, low-risk documentation skill. No network egress, no credential access, no destructive or obfuscated actions, and no attempts to manipulate the reviewer. Minor points: it points to an unpinned GitHub dev-branch zipball as an alternate install source, and it doesn't spell out negative triggers or failure-path guidance (e.g. bad signal quality, missing p

highlights.summary: Line 278 offers installing straight from `https://github.com/neuropsychology/NeuroKit/zipball/dev` (an unpinned dev branch) alongside the normal PyPI install (line 273) — a user who follows this gets
No explicit 'do not use for X' guidance anywhere in the file, so a user can't quickly tell when this skill is the wrong tool (e.g. non-physiological time series, real-time streaming constraints).
No section addresses failure paths — e.g. what happens with noisy signals, missing R-peaks, unsupported sampling rates, or how errors surface to the caller.
